TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi beforefieldinit BooleanSwitch extends System.Diagnostics.Switch

Docs.summary: Provides a simple on/off switch that controls debugging and tracing output.

remarks.format:
## Remarks You can use a Boolean trace switch to enable or disable messages based on their importance. Use the <xref:System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch.Enabled> property to get the current value of the switch.
You can create a <xref:System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch> in your code and set the <xref:System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch.Enabled> property directly to instrument a specific section of code.
The <xref:System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch.Enabled> property of the new switch is set to `false` by default.
You must enable tracing or debugging to use a switch.
> [!NOTE] > These debug and trace compiler switches are not required when using the <xref:System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch> class in isolation. They are only required in conjunction with <xref:System.Diagnostics.Trace> or <xref:System.Diagnostics.Debug> methods that are conditionally compiled.
For more information on instrumenting your application, see <xref:System.Diagnostics.Debug> and <xref:System.Diagnostics.Trace>
> [!NOTE] > To improve performance, you can make <xref:System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch> members `static` in your class.
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch> and uses the switch to determine whether to print an error message. You create the switch at the class level. The `Main` method passes its location to `MyMethod`, which prints an error message and where the error occurred.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Diagnostics/BooleanSwitch/Overview/source.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Diagnostics/BooleanSwitch/Overview/source.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

public BooleanSwitch (string displayName, string description);

Docs.param: The name to display on a user interface.
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified display name and description.

remarks.format:
## Remarks When you create a <xref:System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch>, the <xref:System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch.Enabled> property is set to `false` (disabled).
## Examples The following example creates a <xref:System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch> and uses the switch to determine whether to print an error message. The switch is created at the class level. The `Main` method passes its location to `MyMethod`, which prints an error message and where the error occurred.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Diagnostics/BooleanSwitch/Overview/source.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Diagnostics/BooleanSwitch/Overview/source.vb" id="Snippet1":::

MemberSignature.Value: public BooleanSwitch (string displayName, string? description, string defaultSwitchValue);
.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(string displayName, string description, string defaultSwitchValue) cil managed
Public Sub New (displayName As String, description As String, defaultSwitchValue As String)
new System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch : string * string * string -> System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch
public: BooleanSwitch(System::String ^ displayName, System::String ^ description, System::String ^ defaultSwitchValue);
public BooleanSwitch (string displayName, string description, string defaultSwitchValue);

Docs.param: The name to display on the user interface.
The default value of the switch.
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified display name, description, and default switch value.

MemberSignature.Value: public bool Enabled { get; set; }
.property instance bool Enabled
Public Property Enabled As Boolean
member this.Enabled : bool with get, set
public: property bool Enabled { bool get(); void set(bool value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the switch is enabled or disabled.
Docs.value: if the switch is enabled; otherwise, . The default is .

remarks.format:
## Remarks By default, this field is set to `false` (disabled). To enable the switch, assign this field the value of `true`. To disable the switch, assign the value to `false`. The value of this property is determined by the value of the base class property <xref:System.Diagnostics.Switch.SwitchSetting*>.
## Examples The following code example creates a <xref:System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch> and uses the switch to determine whether to print an error message. The switch is created at the class level. The `Main` method passes its location to `MyMethod`, which prints an error message and the location where the error occurred.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Diagnostics/BooleanSwitch/Enabled/source.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Diagnostics/BooleanSwitch/Enabled/source.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.exception: The caller does not have the correct permission.

MemberSignature.Value: protected override void OnValueChanged ();
.method familyhidebysig virtual instance void OnValueChanged() cil managed
Protected Overrides Sub OnValueChanged ()
override this.OnValueChanged : unit -> unit
protected: override void OnValueChanged();

Docs.summary: Determines whether the new value of the property can be parsed as a Boolean value.

remarks.format: ## Remarks The <xref:System.Diagnostics.BooleanSwitch.OnValueChanged*> method determines whether the new value is a valid string representation of a Boolean value ("false" or "true"). If so, the method sets the <xref:System.Diagnostics.Switch.SwitchSetting> property to 0 or 1. Otherwise, the base method is called, which converts the string value to an integer value, which is then used to set the <xref:System.Diagnostics.Switch.SwitchSetting> property.
